behavior_summary: When in_valid=1, the 4-bit in bus is interpreted as an integer 0–14 and the corresponding bit of the 15-bit out vector is set to 1 while all other bits are 0. When in_valid=0, the out vector is forced to 15'b0 regardless of in. clk and rst exist only for internal assertions and have no effect on the combinational encoding.
edge_cases: in values above 14 (specifically 15) while in_valid=1 produce undefined behavior; the correct response is unspecified.
When in_valid=0, out is always zero even if in holds a valid value.
clk and rst do not gate or register outputs, so any assumption that they synchronize behavior would be incorrect.
priorities: in_valid gating dominates: in_valid=0 forces out=0 regardless of in value.
